sanskrit:
अथ वा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्॥
transliteration:
atha vā bahunaitena kiṃ jñātena tavārjuna |
viṣṭabhyāham idaṃ kṛtsnam ekāṃśena sthito jagat ||
literal_meaning: What is the use of all this detailed knowledge for you, Arjuna? I support this entire universe by a single fragment of Myself.
contextual_meaning: After a dazzling catalogue of divine glories, Krishna collapses the whole display into one breath: the cosmos is only a sliver of Him. The mind that was collecting data is invited to drop the search and rest in the simple, overwhelming fact that everything already rests in Being.
psychological_state: Post-insight cognitive decompression—like closing 50 browser tabs and realizing the answer was never in the tabs but in the one who opened them.
trigger_scenario: You’ve spent weeks comparing every smartphone spec, then someone says, ‘Pick any; they all run on the same electricity.’ The comparison frenzy dissolves into effortless choice.
anchor_text: The whole universe is held in a single breath of Being.
